case_id: ames_raw from AmesHousing R package, seed=2024 70/30 split

pi_items.source: Published as 'Ames, Iowa: Alternative to the Boston Housing Data as an End of Semester Regression Project' in Journal of Statistics Education, Vol 19, No 3, 2011. DOI: 10.1080/10691898.2011.11889627. Full text at https://jse.amstat.org/v19n3/decock.pdf

pi_items.verified_value: AmesHousing R package v0.0.4 (CRAN), authored by Max Kuhn
pi_items.source: CRAN page: https://cran.r-project.org/package=AmesHousing. Confirmed installed and functional in live environment via R --vanilla --slave -e 'requireNamespace("AmesHousing")' returning TRUE. data(ames_raw) loads 2930x82 data frame.
Live environment verification: wc -l returned 2052 lines (header + 2051 data rows). head -1 shows columns starting with MSSubClass,MSZoning,LotFrontage,...
Live environment verification: wc -l returned 880 lines (header + 879 data rows). Same column structure as train except SalePrice column is absent.
Live environment verification: wc -l returned 880 lines. head -2 shows 'Id,SalePrice' header and first row '1,215000'. File is hidden (dot-prefix) and not accessible to the agent.

Independently verified achievable: a 10-predictor linear model on log(SalePrice) with OverallQual, GrLivArea, YearBuilt, TotalBsmtSF, FullBath, LotArea, OverallCond, KitchenQual, Fireplaces, ScreenPorch achieves RMSLE=0.1466 on the test set with all VIF<5. Threshold of 0.16 is achievable but requires proper feature selection and NA handling.

Independently verified using car::vif() in live environment. The 10-predictor model above has max VIF=4.0 (for KitchenQual levels). Standard multicollinearity threshold in statistics literature is VIF<5 or VIF<10.

Independently verified achievable: the 10-predictor model achieves Adj R-squared=0.8446 on training data, exceeding the 0.82 threshold. A simpler 5-predictor model would likely get ~0.80, making this gate non-trivial but achievable with good feature selection.

Independently verified using cooks.distance() in live environment. A 3-predictor model (OverallQual+GrLivArea+GarageCars) with complete cases has max Cook's D=0.727, below the 1.0 threshold. Standard influential observation threshold in regression literature is Cook's D > 1.0 (or 4/n for stricter checks).
Verified in setup_task.sh line 67: set.seed(2024). Ran the split twice in live environment and got identical results (2051 train / 879 test rows both times), confirming determinism.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 69: round(0.7 * n). With n=2930 (after removing Order/PID and NA SalePrice rows), round(0.7*2930)=2051. 2930-2051=879. Matches observed train/test sizes exactly.
Verified: export_result.sh line 10 reads from this exact path. File writing at this path confirmed working via R write.csv() test in live environment.
Verified: export_result.sh line 11 reads from this exact path. Expected columns: test_name, statistic, threshold, pass.
Verified: export_result.sh line 12 reads from this exact path. Expected columns: term, estimate, std_error, p_value.
Verified: export_result.sh line 13 reads from this exact path. PNG writing confirmed working via R png()/dev.off() test in live environment.
Verified: setup_task.sh creates this file at line 112. export_result.sh checks it at line 14. Confirmed visible in RStudio editor via screenshot.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real Ames Housing dataset (Dean De Cock, 2011, Journal of Statistics Education) loaded from the AmesHousing CRAN package via data(ames_raw). After removing Order and PID identifier columns and cleaning column names to CamelCase, the data is split 70/30 with set.seed(2024), yielding exactly 2051 training rows (81 columns including SalePrice) and 879 test rows (80 columns, SalePrice withheld). The training SalePrice ranges from $13,100 to $745,000. The dataset has significant missing values with semantic meaning: PoolQC is 99.5% NA (2041/2051, means no pool), GarageType has 110 NAs (means no garage), LotFrontage has 347 NAs (genuinely missing, requires imputation), and Alley has ~93% NA (means no alley). Column names after cleaning include MSSubClass, MSZoning, LotFrontage, LotArea, OverallQual, GrLivArea, YearBuilt, TotalBsmtSF, GarageCars, GarageArea, etc. A reference model using log(SalePrice) ~ OverallQual + GrLivArea + YearBuilt + TotalBsmtSF + FullBath + LotArea + OverallCond + KitchenQual + Fireplaces + ScreenPorch achieves RMSLE=0.1466 on the test set, Adj R-squared=0.8446, and max VIF=4.0, demonstrating all four quality gates (RMSLE<0.16, VIF<5, Adj R²>0.82, Cook's D<1.0) are simultaneously achievable. Key collinear feature pairs that will cause VIF>5 if both included: GarageCars/GarageArea (r≈0.88), GrLivArea/TotRmsAbvGrd (r≈0.83), TotalBsmtSF/X1stFlrSF (r≈0.82). The agent must handle NAs intelligently (na.omit loses ~65% of rows), use log(SalePrice) as the response for RMSLE optimization, and iteratively select features to balance accuracy against the VIF constraint. Required deliverables are ames_predictions.csv (Id, predicted_price in dollar scale, 879 rows), ames_diagnostics.csv (test_name, statistic, threshold, pass), ames_coefficients.csv (term, estimate, std_error, p_value), and ames_diagnostic_plots.png (2x2 panel: residuals vs fitted, Q-Q, Scale-Location, Cook's distance). Ground truth for RMSLE verification is stored at /tmp/.ames_ground_truth.csv with columns Id and SalePrice. The export_result.sh script independently computes RMSLE from predictions against ground truth as an anti-gaming measure. All required R packages (car for vif(), lmtest for bptest(), Metrics, ggplot2, dplyr, MASS) are pre-installed.
